semantic_status: Predeclared environmental predictor registry for association analyses of fully automated model-derived trait measurements. The registry does not confer causal interpretation.

dataset.name: CHELSA V2.1 bioclim
dataset.spatial_resolution_claim: Source dataset is distributed as CHELSA global bioclim GeoTIFFs; native grid metadata are recorded at extraction time rather than hard-coded here.

analysis_role.primary_predictor_set: Four predeclared macroclimate axes; no post-hoc variable mining is permitted in the primary association analysis.
analysis_role.spatial_coordinates: Latitude and longitude are retained for spatial blocking and sensitivity analysis, not treated as substitutes for climate in the primary predictor set.
analysis_role.topography_soils: Excluded from the initial primary predictor set and reserved for separately declared sensitivity analyses.

predictors.value_handling: Extract source-native raster values and record scale, offset, nodata, units, and tags. Primary model code standardizes extracted numeric values without assuming undocumented unit transformations.
Extract source-native raster values and record scale, offset, nodata, units, and tags. Primary model code standardizes extracted numeric values without assuming undocumented unit transformations.
Extract source-native raster values and record scale, offset, nodata, units, and tags. Primary model code standardizes extracted numeric values without assuming undocumented unit transformations.
Extract source-native raster values and record scale, offset, nodata, units, and tags. Primary model code standardizes extracted numeric values without assuming undocumented unit transformations.

coordinate_policy.environmental_coordinate_requirement: Use only observations already marked coordinate_usable_for_environment=true.
coordinate_policy.primary_model_precision_rule: Do not silently discard observations by positional accuracy at environmental extraction. Export precision tier and run a <=10 km sensitivity analysis before interpretation.

spatial_policy.rule: Use spatial blocks for grouped validation and residual diagnostics; do not infer independence from multiple observations in the same block.
